{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b7c0089108374e4e886dfbbd","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Mode C worker loop for processing chunked background jobs (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The worker continuously polls for queued jobs, claims them, processes them,\n4\t//! and renews claims. Large jobs are split into chunks; chunk jobs execute\n5\t//! the actual work (dump import, reshard backfill).\n6\t\n7\t#[cfg(test)]\n8\tmod acceptance_tests;\n9\t\n10\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n11\tuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{ClaimedJob, JobChunk, JobParams, JobProgress, JobType, ModeCCoordinator};\n12\tuse crate::reshard_chunking;\n13\tuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\n14\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n15\tuse std::time::Duration;\n16\tuse tokio::time::interval;\n17\tuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n18\t\n19\t/// Mode C worker configuration.\n20\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n21\tpub struct ModeCWorkerConfig {\n22\t    /// Poll interval for claiming new jobs.\n23\t    pub poll_interval_ms: u64,\n24\t    /// Heartbeat interval for renewing claims.\n25\t    pub heartbeat_interval_ms: u64,\n26\t    /// Maximum concurrent jobs per worker.\n27\t    pub max_concurrent_jobs: usize,\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\timpl Default for ModeCWorkerConfig {\n31\t    fn default() -> Self {\n32\t        Self {\n33\t            poll_interval_ms: 1000,      // 1 second\n34\t            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10000, // 10 seconds\n35\t            max_concurrent_jobs: 3,\n36\t        }\n37\t    }\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Mode C worker for processing background jobs.\n41\tpub struct ModeCWorker {\n42\t    /// Mode C coordinator.\n43\t    coordinator: ModeCCoordinator,\n44\t    /// Worker configuration.\n45\t    config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n46\t    /// Currently running jobs.\n47\t    running_jobs: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<Vec<RunningJob>>>,\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t/// A job currently being processed by this worker.\n51\tstruct RunningJob {\n52\t    /// Job ID.\n53\t    id: String,\n54\t    /// Job type.\n55\t    type_: JobType,\n56\t    /// Job parameters.\n57\t    params: JobParams,\n58\t    /// Last heartbeat time.\n59\t    last_heartbeat: i64,\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\timpl ModeCWorker {\n63\t    /// Create a new Mode C worker.\n64\t    pub fn new(\n65\t        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n66\t        pod_id: String,\n67\t        config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n68\t    ) -> Self {\n69\t        let coordinator = ModeCCoordinator::new(task_store, pod_id)\n70\t            .with_claim_ttl_ms(30_000) // 30 seconds\n71\t            .with_heartbeat_interval_ms(config.heartbeat_interval_ms as i64);\n72\t\n73\t        Self {\n74\t            coordinator,\n75\t            config,\n76\t            running_jobs: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n77\t        }\n78\t    }\n79\t\n80\t    /// Start the worker loop.\n81\t    ///\n82\t    /// This runs continuously, polling for jobs and processing them.\n83\t    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<()> {\n84\t        info!(\"Starting Mode C worker loop\");\n85\t\n86\t        let mut poll_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.poll_interval_ms));\n87\t        let mut heartbeat_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.heartbeat_interval_ms));\n88\t\n89\t        loop {\n90\t            tokio::select! {\n91\t                _ = poll_interval.tick() => {\n92\t                    if let Err(e) = self.poll_and_claim().await {\n93\t                        error!(\"Error polling for jobs: {}\", e);\n94\t                    }\n95\t                }\n96\t                _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {\n97\t                    if let Err(e) = self.renew_claims().await {\n98\t                        error!(\"Error renewing claims: {}\", e);\n99\t                    }\n100\t                }\n101\t            }\n102\t        }\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    /// Poll for queued jobs and claim one if available.\n106\t    async fn poll_and_claim(&self) -> Result<()> {\n107\t        // Check if we're at capacity\n108\t        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n109\t        if running.len() >= self.config.max_concurrent_jobs {\n110\t            debug!(\"At capacity ({} jobs), skipping poll\", running.len());\n111\t            return Ok(());\n112\t        }\n113\t        drop(running);\n114\t\n115\t        // Reclaim expired claims first\n116\t        let reclaimed = self.coordinator.reclaim_expired_claims()?;\n117\t        if reclaimed > 0 {\n118\t            info!(\"Reclaimed {} expired job claims\", reclaimed);\n119\t        }\n120\t\n121\t        // Try to claim a job\n122\t        let claimed = match self.coordinator.claim_job()? {\n123\t            Some(job) => job,\n124\t            None => return Ok(()), // No jobs available\n125\t        };\n126\t\n127\t        let job_id = claimed.id.clone();\n128\t        let job_type_str = claimed.type_.clone();\n129\t\n130\t        info!(\"Claimed job {} (type: {})\", job_id, job_type_str);\n131\t\n132\t        // Parse job type and parameters\n133\t        let job_type = JobType::from_str(&claimed.type_)\n134\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"unknown job type: {}\", claimed.type_)))?;\n135\t        let params = claimed.parse_params()?;\n136\t\n137\t        // Check if this is a large job that needs chunking\n138\t        if claimed.parent_job_id.is_none() && self.should_chunk(&job_type, &params) {\n139\t            // Split into chunks and re-enqueue\n140\t            self.split_and_enqueue(&claimed, &job_type, &params).await?;\n141\t            return Ok(());\n142\t        }\n143\t\n144\t        // Spawn a task to process the job\n145\t        let running_job = RunningJob {\n146\t            id: job_id.clone(),\n147\t            type_: job_type,\n148\t            params: params.clone(),\n149\t            last_heartbeat: crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms(),\n150\t        };\n151\t\n152\t        {\n153\t            let mut running = self.running_jobs.write().await;\n154\t            running.push(running_job);\n155\t        }\n156\t\n157\t        let coordinator = self.coordinator.clone();\n158\t        let running_jobs = self.running_jobs.clone();\n159\t        let job_id_clone = job_id.clone();\n160\t\n161\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n162\t            let result = match job_type {\n163\t                JobType::DumpImport => Self::process_dump_import(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n164\t                JobType::ReshardBackfill => Self::process_reshard_backfill(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n165\t            };\n166\t\n167\t            // Remove from running jobs\n168\t            {\n169\t                let mut running = running_jobs.write().await;\n170\t                running.retain(|j| j.id != job_id_clone);\n171\t            }\n172\t\n173\t            if let Err(e) = result {\n174\t                error!(\"Job {} failed: {}\", job_id_clone, e);\n175\t                let progress = JobProgress::default();\n176\t                let _ = coordinator.fail_job(&job_id_clone, &progress, e.to_string());\n177\t            }\n178\t        });\n179\t\n180\t        Ok(())\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Renew claims for all running jobs.\n184\t    async fn renew_claims(&self) -> Result<()> {\n185\t        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n186\t        let now = crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms();\n187\t\n188\t        for job in running.iter() {\n189\t            match self.coordinator.renew_claim(&job.id) {\n190\t                Ok(true) => {\n191\t                    debug!(\"Renewed claim for job {}\", job.id);\n192\t                }\n193\t                Ok(false) => {\n194\t                    warn!(\"Failed to renew claim for job {} - may have lost ownership\", job.id);\n195\t                }\n196\t                Err(e) => {\n197\t                    error!(\"Error renewing claim for job {}: {}\", job.id, e);\n198\t                }\n199\t            }\n200\t        }\n201\t\n202\t        Ok(())\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    /// Check if a job should be split into chunks.\n206\t    fn should_chunk(&self, job_type: &JobType, params: &JobParams) -> bool {\n207\t        const DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES: u64 = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n208\t\n209\t        match job_type {\n210\t            JobType::DumpImport => {\n211\t                // Chunk if source size exceeds 2x the default chunk size\n212\t                if let Some(size) = params.source_size_bytes {\n213\t                    size > DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES * 2\n214\t                } else {\n215\t                    false\n216\t                }\n217\t            }\n218\t            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n219\t                // Chunk if old_shards exceeds 32 (configurable threshold)\n220\t                if let Some(old_shards) = params.old_shards {\n221\t                    old_shards > 32\n222\t                } else {\n223\t                    false\n224\t                }\n225\t            }\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n230\t    async fn split_and_enqueue(\n231\t        &self,\n232\t        claimed: &ClaimedJob,\n233\t        job_type: &JobType,\n234\t        params: &JobParams,\n235\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n236\t        let chunk_size_bytes = self.coordinator.default_chunk_size_bytes();\n237\t\n238\t        let chunks = match job_type {\n239\t            JobType::DumpImport => {\n240\t                // For dump import, split on byte offset boundaries\n241\t                // In a full implementation, we would:\n242\t                // 1. Fetch the dump data from params.source_url\n243\t                // 2. Use dump_chunking::split_dump_into_chunks to split on NDJSON line boundaries\n244\t                // For now, we create size-based chunks that will be aligned to line boundaries\n245\t                // during actual processing by the worker that processes each chunk\n246\t                let source_size = params.source_size_bytes.unwrap_or(0);\n247\t                if source_size == 0 {\n248\t                    return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"source_size_bytes is required for dump import chunking\".into()));\n249\t                }\n250\t\n251\t                // Calculate number of chunks (ceiling division)\n252\t                let total_chunks = ((source_size + chunk_size_bytes - 1) / chunk_size_bytes) as u32;\n253\t\n254\t                (0..total_chunks)\n255\t                    .map(|i| {\n256\t                        let i = i as u64;\n257\t                        let start = i * chunk_size_bytes;\n258\t                        let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size_bytes, source_size);\n259\t                        JobChunk {\n260\t                            index: i as u32,\n261\t                            total: total_chunks,\n262\t                            start: start.to_string(),\n263\t                            end: end.to_string(),\n264\t                            size_bytes: end - start,\n265\t                        }\n266\t                    })\n267\t                    .collect()\n268\t            }\n269\t            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n270\t                // Use reshard_chunking to split by shard-id range\n271\t                let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(1);\n272\t                let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(old_shards * 2);\n273\t                let shards_per_chunk = 32; // Configurable shard count per chunk\n274\t\n275\t                let specs = reshard_chunking::split_reshard_into_chunks(\n276\t                    old_shards,\n277\t                    target_shards,\n278\t                    shards_per_chunk,\n279\t                );\n280\t                reshard_chunking::reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs)\n281\t            }\n282\t        };\n283\t\n284\t        info!(\"Splitting job {} into {} chunks\", claimed.id, chunks.len());\n285\t        self.coordinator.split_job_into_chunks(claimed, chunks)?;\n286\t\n287\t        Ok(())\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    /// Process a dump import job.\n291\t    async fn process_dump_import(\n292\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n293\t        job_id: &str,\n294\t        params: &JobParams,\n295\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n296\t        info!(\"Processing dump import job {}\", job_id);\n297\t\n298\t        // If this is a chunk job, process the chunk\n299\t        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n300\t            info!(\n301\t                \"Processing dump chunk {}/{} (offsets {}-{})\",\n302\t                chunk.index,\n303\t                chunk.total,\n304\t                chunk.start,\n305\t                chunk.end\n306\t            );\n307\t\n308\t            // Parse chunk boundaries\n309\t            let start_offset: u64 = chunk.start.parse()\n310\t                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk start offset\".into()))?;\n311\t            let end_offset: u64 = chunk.end.parse()\n312\t                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk end offset\".into()))?;\n313\t\n314\t            // Process the dump import chunk\n315\t            let result = Self::process_dump_chunk(\n316\t                coordinator,\n317\t                job_id,\n318\t                params,\n319\t                start_offset,\n320\t                end_offset,\n321\t            ).await;\n322\t\n323\t            match result {\n324\t                Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor)) => {\n325\t                    let progress = JobProgress {\n326\t                        bytes_processed,\n327\t                        docs_routed,\n328\t                        last_cursor,\n329\t                        error: None,\n330\t                    };\n331\t                    coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n332\t                    info!(\n333\t                        \"Completed dump chunk {}/{}: {} bytes, {} docs\",\n334\t                        chunk.index, chunk.total, bytes_processed, docs_routed\n335\t                    );\n336\t                }\n337\t                Err(e) => {\n338\t                    let progress = JobProgress {\n339\t                        bytes_processed: start_offset,\n340\t                        docs_routed: 0,\n341\t                        last_cursor: start_offset.to_string(),\n342\t                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n343\t                    };\n344\t                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n345\t                    return Err(e);\n346\t                }\n347\t            }\n348\t        } else {\n349\t            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n350\t            let progress = JobProgress {\n351\t                bytes_processed: 0,\n352\t                docs_routed: 0,\n353\t                last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n354\t                error: None,\n355\t            };\n356\t\n357\t            coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n358\t        }\n359\t\n360\t        info!(\"Completed dump import job {}\", job_id);\n361\t\n362\t        Ok(())\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    /// Process a single dump chunk.\n366\t    ///\n367\t    /// This is a simulation of the full dump import processing. In production,\n368\t    /// this would:\n369\t    /// 1. Fetch dump data from params.source_url with Range header\n370\t    /// 2. Parse NDJSON lines (align to line boundaries)\n371\t    /// 3. Route each document to target shard based on primary_key\n372\t    /// 4. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n373\t    /// 5. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n374\t    async fn process_dump_chunk(\n375\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n376\t        job_id: &str,\n377\t        params: &JobParams,\n378\t        start_offset: u64,\n379\t        end_offset: u64,\n380\t    ) -> Result<(u64, u64, String)> {\n381\t        use crate::router::shard_for_key;\n382\t        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n383\t\n384\t        let chunk_size = end_offset - start_offset;\n385\t        let shard_count = params.shard_count.unwrap_or(64);\n386\t        let primary_key = params.primary_key.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"id\");\n387\t\n388\t        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n389\t        let batch_size = 10_000_000; // 10 MB batches\n390\t        let mut bytes_processed = 0u64;\n391\t        let mut docs_routed = 0u64;\n392\t        let mut last_cursor = start_offset.to_string();\n393\t\n394\t        let start_time = Instant::now();\n395\t        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n396\t\n397\t        while bytes_processed < chunk_size {\n398\t            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(bytes_processed + batch_size, chunk_size);\n399\t            let batch_bytes = batch_end - bytes_processed;\n400\t\n401\t            // Simulate processing time based on batch size\n402\t            // In production, this would be actual I/O and processing\n403\t            let simulate_delay = Duration::from_millis((batch_bytes / 1_000_000) * 10);\n404\t            tokio::time::sleep(simulate_delay).await;\n405\t\n406\t            // Simulate document routing\n407\t            // Estimate ~100KB per document (typical JSON document)\n408\t            let estimated_docs = batch_bytes / 100_000;\n409\t            for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n410\t                // Simulate document key\n411\t                let doc_key = format!(\"{}-{}-{}\", primary_key, start_offset + bytes_processed, i);\n412\t                let _shard_id = shard_for_key(&doc_key, shard_count);\n413\t                // In production, we would route the document to the target node/shard\n414\t            }\n415\t\n416\t            bytes_processed = batch_end;\n417\t            docs_routed += estimated_docs;\n418\t            last_cursor = (start_offset + bytes_processed).to_string();\n419\t\n420\t            // Update progress periodically\n421\t            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n422\t            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n423\t                // Update progress and renew claim\n424\t                let progress = JobProgress {\n425\t                    bytes_processed,\n426\t                    docs_routed,\n427\t                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n428\t                    error: None,\n429\t                };\n430\t                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n431\t\n432\t                // Renew the claim\n433\t                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n434\t\n435\t                // Reset the timer\n436\t                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n437\t            }\n438\t        }\n439\t\n440\t        Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor))\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    /// Process a reshard backfill job.\n444\t    async fn process_reshard_backfill(\n445\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n446\t        job_id: &str,\n447\t        params: &JobParams,\n448\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n449\t        info!(\"Processing reshard backfill job {}\", job_id);\n450\t\n451\t        // If this is a chunk job, process the shard range\n452\t        if let Some(chunk) = In production,\n518\t    /// this would:\n519\t    /// 1. Read documents from old shard range [start_shard, end_shard)\n520\t    /// 2. Re-route each document to new shard configuration\n521\t    /// 3. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n522\t    /// 4. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n523\t    async fn process_reshard_chunk(\n524\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n525\t        job_id: &str,\n526\t        params: &JobParams,\n527\t        start_shard: u32,\n528\t        end_shard: u32,\n529\t    ) -> Result<(u64, String)> {\n530\t        use crate::router::{shard_for_key, assign_shard_in_group};\n531\t        use crate::topology::{Topology, Group, NodeId};\n532\t        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n533\t\n534\t        let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(64);\n535\t        let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(128);\n536\t        let shard_count = (end_shard - start_shard) as u64;\n537\t\n538\t        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n539\t        let batch_shards = 4; // Process 4 shards at a time\n540\t        let mut shards_processed = 0u32;\n541\t        let mut docs_backfilled = 0u64;\n542\t        let mut last_cursor = start_shard.to_string();\n543\t\n544\t        let start_time = Instant::now();\n545\t        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n546\t\n547\t        // Create a simple topology for routing simulation\n548\t        let topology = Topology::new(2, 1, 3); // 2 groups, 1 RF, 3 nodes per group\n549\t\n550\t        while start_shard + shards_processed < end_shard {\n551\t            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(start_shard + shards_processed + batch_shards, end_shard);\n552\t            let batch_count = (batch_end - (start_shard + shards_processed)) as u64;\n553\t\n554\t            // Simulate document processing for each shard\n555\t            // Estimate ~1000 documents per shard\n556\t            for shard_id in (start_shard + shards_processed)..batch_end {\n557\t                let estimated_docs = 1000u64;\n558\t\n559\t                for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n560\t                    // Simulate document key\n561\t                    let doc_key = format!(\"doc-shard{}-{}\", shard_id, i);\n562\t\n563\t                    // Compute old shard assignment\n564\t                    let _old_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, old_shards);\n565\t\n566\t                    // Compute new shard assignment\n567\t                    let new_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, target_shards);\n568\t\n569\t                    // In production, we would:\n570\t                    // 1. Read the document from the old shard\n571\t                    // 2. Write it to the new shard assignment\n572\t                    // 3. Handle any conflicts or duplicates\n573\t\n574\t                    // Simulate routing to replica groups\n575\t                    for group in topology.groups() {\n576\t                        let _targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n577\t                        // In production, we would write to these target nodes\n578\t                    }\n579\t                }\n580\t\n581\t                docs_backfilled += estimated_docs;\n582\t            }\n583\t\n584\t            shards_processed = batch_end - start_shard;\n585\t            last_cursor = (start_shard + shards_processed as u32).to_string();\n586\t\n587\t            // Update progress periodically\n588\t            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n589\t            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n590\t                // Update progress and renew claim\n591\t                let progress = JobProgress {\n592\t                    bytes_processed: 0,\n593\t                    docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n594\t                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n595\t                    error: None,\n596\t                };\n597\t                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n598\t\n599\t                // Renew the claim\n600\t                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n601\t\n602\t                // Reset the timer\n603\t                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n604\t            }\n605\t\n606\t            // Simulate processing time\n607\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n608\t        }\n609\t\n610\t        Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor))\n611\t    }\n612\t}\n613\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c1cf07d-34ce-408c-9cc1-e760c6668378","uuid":"9e008e57-cb14-4e28-9af1-94b47f0fcadb","timestamp":"2026-05-23T11:07:20.869Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/mode_c_worker/mod.rs","content":"//! Mode C worker loop for processing chunked background jobs (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! The worker continuously polls for queued jobs, claims them, processes them,\n//! and renews claims. Large jobs are split into chunks; chunk jobs execute\n//! the actual work (dump import, reshard backfill).\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod acceptance_tests;\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{ClaimedJob, JobChunk, JobParams, JobProgress, JobType, ModeCCoordinator};\nuse crate::reshard_chunking;\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::interval;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Mode C worker configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ModeCWorkerConfig {\n    /// Poll interval for claiming new jobs.\n    pub poll_interval_ms: u64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval for renewing claims.\n    pub heartbeat_interval_ms: u64,\n    /// Maximum concurrent jobs per worker.\n    pub max_concurrent_jobs: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Default for ModeCWorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            poll_interval_ms: 1000,      // 1 second\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10000, // 10 seconds\n            max_concurrent_jobs: 3,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Mode C worker for processing background jobs.\npub struct ModeCWorker {\n    /// Mode C coordinator.\n    coordinator: ModeCCoordinator,\n    /// Worker configuration.\n    config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n    /// Currently running jobs.\n    running_jobs: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<Vec<RunningJob>>>,\n}\n\n/// A job currently being processed by this worker.\nstruct RunningJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    type_: JobType,\n    /// Job parameters.\n    params: JobParams,\n    /// Last heartbeat time.\n    last_heartbeat: i64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCWorker {\n    /// Create a new Mode C worker.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n        config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let coordinator = ModeCCoordinator::new(task_store, pod_id)\n            .with_claim_ttl_ms(30_000) // 30 seconds\n            .with_heartbeat_interval_ms(config.heartbeat_interval_ms as i64);\n\n        Self {\n            coordinator,\n            config,\n            running_jobs: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the worker loop.\n    ///\n    /// This runs continuously, polling for jobs and processing them.\n    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Starting Mode C worker loop\");\n\n        let mut poll_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.poll_interval_ms));\n        let mut heartbeat_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.heartbeat_interval_ms));\n\n        loop {\n            tokio::select! {\n                _ = poll_interval.tick() => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.poll_and_claim().await {\n                        error!(\"Error polling for jobs: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.renew_claims().await {\n                        error!(\"Error renewing claims: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll for queued jobs and claim one if available.\n    async fn poll_and_claim(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        // Check if we're at capacity\n        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n        if running.len() >= self.config.max_concurrent_jobs {\n            debug!(\"At capacity ({} jobs), skipping poll\", running.len());\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n        drop(running);\n\n        // Reclaim expired claims first\n        let reclaimed = self.coordinator.reclaim_expired_claims()?;\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\"Reclaimed {} expired job claims\", reclaimed);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim a job\n        let claimed = match self.coordinator.claim_job()? {\n            Some(job) => job,\n            None => return Ok(()), // No jobs available\n        };\n\n        let job_id = claimed.id.clone();\n        let job_type_str = claimed.type_.clone();\n\n        info!(\"Claimed job {} (type: {})\", job_id, job_type_str);\n\n        // Parse job type and parameters\n        let job_type = JobType::from_str(&claimed.type_)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"unknown job type: {}\", claimed.type_)))?;\n        let params = claimed.parse_params()?;\n\n        // Check if this is a large job that needs chunking\n        if claimed.parent_job_id.is_none() && self.should_chunk(&job_type, &params) {\n            // Split into chunks and re-enqueue\n            self.split_and_enqueue(&claimed, &job_type, &params).await?;\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a task to process the job\n        let running_job = RunningJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: job_type,\n            params: params.clone(),\n            last_heartbeat: crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms(),\n        };\n\n        {\n            let mut running = self.running_jobs.write().await;\n            running.push(running_job);\n        }\n\n        let coordinator = self.coordinator.clone();\n        let running_jobs = self.running_jobs.clone();\n        let job_id_clone = job_id.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let result = match job_type {\n                JobType::DumpImport => Self::process_dump_import(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n                JobType::ReshardBackfill => Self::process_reshard_backfill(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n            };\n\n            // Remove from running jobs\n            {\n                let mut running = running_jobs.write().await;\n                running.retain(|j| j.id != job_id_clone);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(e) = result {\n                error!(\"Job {} failed: {}\", job_id_clone, e);\n                let progress = JobProgress::default();\n                let _ = coordinator.fail_job(&job_id_clone, &progress, e.to_string());\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Renew claims for all running jobs.\n    async fn renew_claims(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n        let now = crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms();\n\n        for job in running.iter() {\n            match self.coordinator.renew_claim(&job.id) {\n                Ok(true) => {\n                    debug!(\"Renewed claim for job {}\", job.id);\n                }\n                Ok(false) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to renew claim for job {} - may have lost ownership\", job.id);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"Error renewing claim for job {}: {}\", job.id, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a job should be split into chunks.\n    fn should_chunk(&self, job_type: &JobType, params: &JobParams) -> bool {\n        const DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES: u64 = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n\n        match job_type {\n            JobType::DumpImport => {\n                // Chunk if source size exceeds 2x the default chunk size\n                if let Some(size) = params.source_size_bytes {\n                    size > DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES * 2\n                } else {\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n                // Chunk if old_shards exceeds 32 (configurable threshold)\n                if let Some(old_shards) = params.old_shards {\n                    old_shards > 32\n                } else {\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    async fn split_and_enqueue(\n        &self,\n        claimed: &ClaimedJob,\n        job_type: &JobType,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let chunk_size_bytes = self.coordinator.default_chunk_size_bytes();\n\n        let chunks = match job_type {\n            JobType::DumpImport => {\n                // For dump import, split on byte offset boundaries\n                // In a full implementation, we would:\n                // 1. Fetch the dump data from params.source_url\n                // 2. Use dump_chunking::split_dump_into_chunks to split on NDJSON line boundaries\n                // For now, we create size-based chunks that will be aligned to line boundaries\n                // during actual processing by the worker that processes each chunk\n                let source_size = params.source_size_bytes.unwrap_or(0);\n                if source_size == 0 {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"source_size_bytes is required for dump import chunking\".into()));\n                }\n\n                // Calculate number of chunks (ceiling division)\n                let total_chunks = ((source_size + chunk_size_bytes - 1) / chunk_size_bytes) as u32;\n\n                (0..total_chunks)\n                    .map(|i| {\n                        let i = i as u64;\n                        let start = i * chunk_size_bytes;\n                        let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size_bytes, source_size);\n                        JobChunk {\n                            index: i as u32,\n                            total: total_chunks,\n                            start: start.to_string(),\n                            end: end.to_string(),\n                            size_bytes: end - start,\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .collect()\n            }\n            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n                // Use reshard_chunking to split by shard-id range\n                let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(1);\n                let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(old_shards * 2);\n                let shards_per_chunk = 32; // Configurable shard count per chunk\n\n                let specs = reshard_chunking::split_reshard_into_chunks(\n                    old_shards,\n                    target_shards,\n                    shards_per_chunk,\n                );\n                reshard_chunking::reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs)\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\"Splitting job {} into {} chunks\", claimed.id, chunks.len());\n        self.coordinator.split_job_into_chunks(claimed, chunks)?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a dump import job.\n    async fn process_dump_import(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Processing dump import job {}\", job_id);\n\n        // If this is a chunk job, process the chunk\n        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n            info!(\n                \"Processing dump chunk {}/{} (offsets {}-{})\",\n                chunk.index,\n                chunk.total,\n                chunk.start,\n                chunk.end\n            );\n\n            // Parse chunk boundaries\n            let start_offset: u64 = chunk.start.parse()\n                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk start offset\".into()))?;\n            let end_offset: u64 = chunk.end.parse()\n                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk end offset\".into()))?;\n\n            // Process the dump import chunk\n            let result = Self::process_dump_chunk(\n                coordinator,\n                job_id,\n                params,\n                start_offset,\n                end_offset,\n            ).await;\n\n            match result {\n                Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor)) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed,\n                        docs_routed,\n                        last_cursor,\n                        error: None,\n                    };\n                    coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n                    info!(\n                        \"Completed dump chunk {}/{}: {} bytes, {} docs\",\n                        chunk.index, chunk.total, bytes_processed, docs_routed\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: start_offset,\n                        docs_routed: 0,\n                        last_cursor: start_offset.to_string(),\n                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    };\n                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n            let progress = JobProgress {\n                bytes_processed: 0,\n                docs_routed: 0,\n                last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Completed dump import job {}\", job_id);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a single dump chunk.\n    ///\n    /// This is a simulation of the full dump import processing. In production,\n    /// this would:\n    /// 1. Fetch dump data from params.source_url with Range header\n    /// 2. Parse NDJSON lines (align to line boundaries)\n    /// 3. Route each document to target shard based on primary_key\n    /// 4. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n    /// 5. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n    async fn process_dump_chunk(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n        start_offset: u64,\n        end_offset: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(u64, u64, String)> {\n        use crate::router::shard_for_key;\n        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\n        let chunk_size = end_offset - start_offset;\n        let shard_count = params.shard_count.unwrap_or(64);\n        let primary_key = params.primary_key.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"id\");\n\n        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n        let batch_size = 10_000_000; // 10 MB batches\n        let mut bytes_processed = 0u64;\n        let mut docs_routed = 0u64;\n        let mut last_cursor = start_offset.to_string();\n\n        let start_time = Instant::now();\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n\n        while bytes_processed < chunk_size {\n            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(bytes_processed + batch_size, chunk_size);\n            let batch_bytes = batch_end - bytes_processed;\n\n            // Simulate processing time based on batch size\n            // In production, this would be actual I/O and processing\n            let simulate_delay = Duration::from_millis((batch_bytes / 1_000_000) * 10);\n            tokio::time::sleep(simulate_delay).await;\n\n            // Simulate document routing\n            // Estimate ~100KB per document (typical JSON document)\n            let estimated_docs = batch_bytes / 100_000;\n            for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n                // Simulate document key\n                let doc_key = format!(\"{}-{}-{}\", primary_key, start_offset + bytes_processed, i);\n                let _shard_id = shard_for_key(&doc_key, shard_count);\n                // In production, we would route the document to the target node/shard\n            }\n\n            bytes_processed = batch_end;\n            docs_routed += estimated_docs;\n            last_cursor = (start_offset + bytes_processed).to_string();\n\n            // Update progress periodically\n            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n                // Update progress and renew claim\n                let progress = JobProgress {\n                    bytes_processed,\n                    docs_routed,\n                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n                    error: None,\n                };\n                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n                // Renew the claim\n                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n\n                // Reset the timer\n                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor))\n    }\n\n    /// Process a reshard backfill job.\n    async fn process_reshard_backfill(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Processing reshard backfill job {}\", job_id);\n\n        // If this is a chunk job, process the shard range\n        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n            let (start_shard, end_shard) = reshard_chunking::parse_reshard_chunk(chunk)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid chunk spec: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            info!(\n                \"Processing reshard chunk {}/{} (shards {}-{})\",\n                chunk.index,\n                chunk.total,\n                start_shard,\n                end_shard\n            );\n\n            // Process the reshard backfill chunk\n            let result = Self::process_reshard_chunk(\n                coordinator,\n                job_id,\n                params,\n                start_shard,\n                end_shard,\n            ).await;\n\n            match result {\n                Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor)) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: 0, // Not applicable for reshard\n                        docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n                        last_cursor,\n                        error: None,\n                    };\n                    coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n                    info!(\n                        \"Completed reshard chunk {}/{}: {} docs from shards {}-{}\",\n                        chunk.index, chunk.total, docs_backfilled, start_shard, end_shard\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: 0,\n                        docs_routed: 0,\n                        last_cursor: start_shard.to_string(),\n                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    };\n                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n            let progress = JobProgress {\n                bytes_processed: 0,\n                docs_routed: 0,\n                last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Completed reshard backfill job {}\", job_id);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a single reshard backfill chunk.\n    ///\n    /// This is a simulation of the full reshard backfill processing. In production,\n    /// this would:\n    /// 1. Read documents from old shard range [start_shard, end_shard)\n    /// 2. Re-route each document to new shard configuration\n    /// 3. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n    /// 4. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n    async fn process_reshard_chunk(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n        start_shard: u32,\n        end_shard: u32,\n    ) -> Result<(u64, String)> {\n        use crate::router::{shard_for_key, assign_shard_in_group};\n        use crate::topology::{Topology, Group, NodeId};\n        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\n        let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(64);\n        let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(128);\n        let shard_count = (end_shard - start_shard) as u64;\n\n        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n        let batch_shards = 4; // Process 4 shards at a time\n        let mut shards_processed = 0u32;\n        let mut docs_backfilled = 0u64;\n        let mut last_cursor = start_shard.to_string();\n\n        let start_time = Instant::now();\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n\n        // Create a simple topology for routing simulation\n        let topology = Topology::new(2, 1, 3); // 2 groups, 1 RF, 3 nodes per group\n\n        while start_shard + shards_processed < end_shard {\n            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(start_shard + shards_processed + batch_shards, end_shard);\n            let batch_count = (batch_end - (start_shard + shards_processed)) as u64;\n\n            // Simulate document processing for each shard\n            // Estimate ~1000 documents per shard\n            for shard_id in (start_shard + shards_processed)..batch_end {\n                let estimated_docs = 1000u64;\n\n                for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n                    // Simulate document key\n                    let doc_key = format!(\"doc-shard{}-{}\", shard_id, i);\n\n                    // Compute old shard assignment\n                    let _old_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, old_shards);\n\n                    // Compute new shard assignment\n                    let new_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, target_shards);\n\n                    // In production, we would:\n                    // 1. Read the document from the old shard\n                    // 2. Write it to the new shard assignment\n                    // 3. Handle any conflicts or duplicates\n\n                    // Simulate routing to replica groups\n                    for group in topology.groups() {\n                        let _targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                        // In production, we would write to these target nodes\n                    }\n                }\n\n                docs_backfilled += estimated_docs;\n            }\n\n            shards_processed = batch_end - start_shard;\n            last_cursor = (start_shard + shards_processed as u32).to_string();\n\n            // Update progress periodically\n            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n                // Update progress and renew claim\n                let progress = JobProgress {\n                    bytes_processed: 0,\n                    docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n                    error: None,\n                };\n                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n                // Renew the claim\n                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n\n                // Reset the timer\n                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n            }\n\n            // Simulate processing time\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        }\n\n        Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor))\n    }\n}\n","numLines":613,"startLine":1,"totalLines":613}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_eb4a90b617ea418a9fa8b1e6","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n2\t\n3\t**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n4\t\n5\tMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## Table of Contents\n10\t\n11\t1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n12\t2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n13\t3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n14\t4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n15\t5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n16\t6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n17\t7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n18\t8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n19\t9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n20\t10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n21\t11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n22\t12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n23\t13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n24\t14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n25\t15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n26\t\n27\t---\n28\t\n29\t## 1. Design Principles\n30\t\n31\t1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n32\t2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n33\t3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n34\t4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n35\t5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n36\t6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n37\t7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n38\t8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n39\t\n40\t### Key constraints\n41\t\n42\t- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n43\t- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n44\t- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n45\t- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n46\t\n47\t---\n48\t\n49\t## 2. Architecture\n50\t\n51\t### Core model\n52\t\n53\tMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n54\t\n55\t- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n56\t- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n57\t- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n58\t- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n59\t- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n60\t- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n61\t- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n62\t- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n63\t- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n64\t\n65\t```\n66\tClient\n67\t  │\n68\t  ▼\n69\tMiroir Orchestrator\n70\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n71\t  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n72\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n73\t\n74\t  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n75\t  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n76\t  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n77\t  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n78\t  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n79\t  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t### Replication factor\n83\t\n84\t| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n85\t|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n86\t| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n87\t| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n88\t| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n89\t\n90\t### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n91\t\n92\tMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n93\t\n94\t```\n95\tnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\n96\tassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n97\t                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n98\t\n99\twrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n100\t                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n101\t\n102\tsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\n103\tsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\tWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n107\t\n108\tProperties:\n109\t- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n110\t- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n111\t- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n112\t- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n113\t- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n114\t\n115\tThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n116\t\n117\t### Write path\n118\t\n119\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n120\t2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n121\t3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n122\t4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n123\t5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n124\t6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n125\t7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n126\t8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n127\t\n128\t**Write quorum — two rules.**\n129\t\n130\t1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n131\t2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n132\t\n133\tExamples:\n134\t\n135\t| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n136\t|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n137\t| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n138\t| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n139\t| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n140\t| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n141\t| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n142\t\n143\tDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n144\t\n145\t**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n146\t\n147\t**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n148\t\n149\t### Read path\n150\t\n151\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n152\t2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n153\t3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n154\t4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n155\t5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n156\t6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n157\t7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n158\t8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n159\t9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n160\t\n161\tThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n162\t\n163\t**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n164\t\n165\t**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n166\t\n167\t**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n168\t\n169\t### Replica groups in practice\n170\t\n171\tReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n172\t\n173\t```\n174\tExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n175\t\n176\tGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n177\t  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n178\t  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n179\t  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n180\t  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n181\t  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n182\t  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n183\t\n184\tWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\n185\tQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\n186\tQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\n187\tQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\tWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n191\t\n192\t**Tradeoffs table**\n193\t\n194\t| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n195\t|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n196\t| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n197\t| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n198\t| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n199\t| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n200\t| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4c1cf07d-34ce-408c-9cc1-e760c6668378","uuid":"f47e3b8d-b77e-4c4d-81a4-e5e9c072b805","timestamp":"2026-05-23T11:07:20.871Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n\n**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n\nMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n\n### Key constraints\n\n- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture\n\n### Core model\n\nMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n\n- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n\n```\nClient\n  │\n  ▼\nMiroir Orchestrator\n  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n\n  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n```\n\n### Replication factor\n\n| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n\n### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n\nMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n\n```\nnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\nassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n\nwrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n\nsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\nsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n```\n\nWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n\nProperties:\n- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n\nThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n\n### Write path\n\n1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n\n**Write quorum — two rules.**\n\n1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n\nExamples:\n\n| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n\nDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n\n**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n\n**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n\n### Read path\n\n1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n\nThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n\n**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n\n**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n\n**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n\n### Replica groups in practice\n\nReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n\n```\nExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n\nGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n\nWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\nQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\nQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\nQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n```\n\nWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n\n**Tradeoffs table**\n\n| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":3756}}}

///\n283\t    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n284\t    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n285\t        // List queued jobs\n286\t chunk_count = total_chunks,\n451\t            \"split job into chunks\"\n452\t        );\n453\t\n454\t        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n458\t    ///\n459\t    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n460\t    /// Preserves job progress for idempotent resume.\n461\t    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n462\t        let now = now_ms();\n463\t        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n464\t\n465\t        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n466\t        for job in expired_jobs {\n467\t            // Preserve the existing progress for idempotent resume\n468\t            // The job.progress field contains the last_cursor and other state\n469\t            // needed by the next pod to resume from where the previous pod left off\n470\t            let progress_json = job.progress.clone();\n471\t\n472\t            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n473\t            self.task_store.reclaim_job_claim(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n474\t\n475\t            debug!(\n476\t                job_id = %job.id,\n477\t                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n478\t                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n479\t            );\n480\t\n481\t            reclaimed += 1;\n482\t        }\n483\t\n484\t        if reclaimed > 0 {\n485\t            info!(\n486\t                count = reclaimed,\n487\t                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n488\t            );\n489\t        }\n490\t\n491\t        Ok(reclaimed)\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n495\t    ///\n496\t    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n497\t    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n498\t        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    /// Get job by ID.\n502\t    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n503\t
