Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n//!\n//! ## HPA Queue Depth Metric (plan §14.4)\n//!\n//! The coordinator provides a queue depth metric for Horizontal Pod Autoscaler:\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! miroir:jobs:_queued (Redis set)\n//! SCARD miroir:jobs:_queued = miroir_background_queue_depth\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The HPA can be configured to scale on this external metric:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! metrics:\n//! - type: External\n//!   external:\n//!     metric:\n//!       name: miroir_background_queue_depth\n//!     target:\n//!       type: AverageValue\n//!       averageValue: 10\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Example HPA configuration that scales up when queue depth > 10:\n//! ```yaml\n//! apiVersion: autoscaling/v2\n//! kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler\n//! metadata:\n//!   name: miroir-worker-hpa\n//! spec:\n//!   scaleTargetRef:\n//!     apiVersion: apps/v1\n//!     kind: Deployment\n//!     name: miroir-worker\n//!   minReplicas: 2\n//!   maxReplicas: 10\n//!   metrics:\n//!   - type: External\n//!     external:\n//!       metric:\n//!         name: miroir_background_queue_depth\n//!       target:\n//!         type: AverageValue\n//!         averageValue: 10\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the default chunk size in bytes.\n    pub fn default_chunk_size_bytes(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n            created_at: now_ms(),\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n                created_at: now_ms(),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    /// Preserves job progress for idempotent resume.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Preserve the existing progress for idempotent resume\n            // The job.progress field contains the last_cursor and other state\n            // needed by the next pod to resume from where the previous pod left off\n            let progress_json = job.progress.clone();\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            self.task_store.reclaim_job_claim(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List jobs by state.\n    pub fn list_jobs_by_state(&self, state: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(state)\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim expiration time for a job (test helper).\n    ///\n    /// This allows tests to simulate time passing without actually waiting.\n    /// WARNING: This should only be used in tests!\n    #[cfg(test)]\n    pub fn set_claim_expires_at_for_test(&self, job_id: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<()> {\n        self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, expires_at)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\npub fn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn test_coordinator() -> ModeCCoordinator {\n Streaming routed dump import (plan §13.9).\n//!\n//! Intercepts dump imports and routes each document to the correct shard\n//! instead of broadcasting to all nodes.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::router::{shard_for_key, import configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DumpImportConfig {\n    /// Import mode: \"streaming\" or \"broadcast\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_mode\")]\n    pub mode: String,\n    /// Batch size for per-target POSTs.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_batch_size\")]\n    pub batch_size: u32,\n    /// Parallel target writes.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_parallel\")]\n    pub parallel_target_writes: u32,\n    /// Memory buffer cap (bytes).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_memory_buffer\")]\n    pub memory_buffer_bytes: u64,\n    /// Chunk size for Mode C coordinator.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_chunk_size\")]\n    pub chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nfn default_mode() -> String {\n    \"streaming\".into()\n}\nfn default_batch_size() -> u32 {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_parallel() -> u32 {\n    8\n}\nfn default_memory_buffer() -> u64 {\n    134_217_728 // 128 MiB\n}\nfn default_chunk_size() -> u64 {\n    268_435_456 // 256 MiB\n}\n\nimpl Default for DumpImportConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: default_mode(),\n            batch_size: default_batch_size(),\n            parallel_target_writes: default_parallel(),\n            memory_buffer_bytes: default_memory_buffer(),\n            chunk_size_bytes: default_chunk_size(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dump import phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum DumpImportPhase {\n    /// No import in progress.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Reading and parsing dump.\n    Reading = 1,\n    /// Routing documents to target nodes.\n    Routing = 2,\n    /// Applying index settings.\n    ApplyingSettings = 3,\n    /// Completed successfully.\n    Complete = 4,\n    /// Failed with error.\n    Failed = 5,\n}\n\n/// Dump import status.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DumpImportStatus {\n    /// Import ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Target index UID.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    /// Documents processed so far.\n    pub documents_processed: u64,\n    /// Total   active_imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    /// Topology for routing.\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl DumpImportManager {\n    /// Create a new dump import manager.\n    pub fn new(config: DumpImportConfig, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            active_imports: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            topology,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start a streaming dump import.\n    pub async fn start_import(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        if self.config.mode != \"streaming\" {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\n                \"streaming dump import is disabled\".into(),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let import_id = format!(\"dump-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let now = millis_now();\n\n        // Create initial status\n        let status = DumpImportStatus {\n            id: import_id.clone(),\n            index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n            phase: DumpImportPhase::Reading,\n            documents_processed: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            bytes_read: 0,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        {\n            let mut imports = self.active_imports.write().await;\n            imports.insert(import_id.clone(), status);\n        }\n\n        // Clone import_id before moving into the async block\n        let import_id_for_spawn = import_id.clone();\n\n        // Spawn background import task\n        let imports = self.active_imports.clone();\n        let topology = self.topology.clone();\n        let config = self.config.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = Self::run_import(\n                &import_id_for_spawn,\n                index_uid,\n                dump_data,\n                primary_key,\n                shard_count,\n                topology,\n                config,\n                imports,\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                tracing::error!(\"Dump import {} failed: {}\", import_id_for_spawn, e);\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(import_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the status of an import.\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, import_id: &str) -> Option<DumpImportStatus> {\n        let imports = self.active_imports.read().await;\n        imports.get(import_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Run the import pipeline.\n    async fn run_import(\n        import_id: &str,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n        topology: Arc<Topology>,\n        config: DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // Update phase to reading\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Reading).await;\n\n        // Parse NDJSON and route documents\n        let data_str = std::str::from_utf8(&dump_data)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid UTF-8 in dump: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Per-target buffers\n        let mut per_target_buffers: HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>> =\n            HashMap::new();\n\n        let mut processed = 0u64;\n        let _total_estimate = 0u64;\n\n        for line in data_str.lines() {\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid JSON in dump: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            // Extract primary key value\n            let pk_value = doc\n                .get(&primary_key)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .ok_or_else(|| {\n                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                        \"missing or invalid primary key field: {}\",\n                        primary_key\n                    ))\n                })?;\n\n            // Compute shard and route\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n\n            // Get target nodes for this shard (assign across all replica groups)\n            let target_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = topology\n                .groups()\n                .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n                .collect();\n\n            if target_nodes.is_empty() {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"no nodes for shard {}\", shard_id)));\n            }\n\n            // Add to each target's buffer\n            for node in &target_nodes {\n                per_target_buffers\n                    .entry((node.clone(), shard_id))\n                    .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                    .push(doc.clone());\n            }\n\n            processed += 1;\n\n            // Flush buffers when they reach batch size\n            if processed % config.batch_size as u64 == 0 {\n                Self::flush_buffers(\n                    &index_uid,\n                    &mut per_target_buffers,\n                    &config,\n                    &imports,\n                    import_id,\n                    processed,\n                )\n                .await?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final flush\n        Self::flush_buffers(\n            &index_uid,\n            &mut per_target_buffers,\n            &config,\n            &imports,\n            import_id,\n            processed,\n        )\n        .await?;\n\n        // Mark complete\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Complete).await;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush buffered documents to target nodes.\n    async fn flush_buffers(\n        index_uid: &str,\n        buffers: &mut HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>>,\n        _config: &DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        processed: u64,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        for ((node, _shard), docs) in buffers.drain() {\n            if docs.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // POST documents to the node\n            // In a real implementation, this would use the HTTP client\n            tracing::debug!(\n                \"Flushing {} documents to node {} for index {}\",\n                docs.len(),\n                node,\n                index_uid\n            );\n\n            // Update status\n            let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n            if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n                status.documents_processed = processed;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the phase of an import.\n    async fn update_phase(\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    ) {\n        let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n        if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n            status.phase = phase;\n            status.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\nimpl the job\n66\t    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n67\t    assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n68\t    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n69\t\n70\t    // test_acceptance_claim_expires_after_30s() {\n106\t    // Acceptance: Kill a claimant mid-chunk: claim expires in 30s;\n107\t    // another pod picks up and resumes at last_cursor\n108\t    let store = Mode C acceptance tests (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\n/// Create a test coordinator with an in-memory store.\nfn test_coordinator(pod_id: &str) -> ModeCCoordinator {\n    let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n    ModeCCoordinator::new(store, pod_id.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Create a test coordinator with a shared store.\nfn dump_import_params(source_size_bytes: u64) -> JobParams {\n    JobParams {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        primary_key: Some(\"id\".to_string()),\n        shard_count: Some(64),\n        old_shards: None,\n        target_shards: None,\n        shadow_index: None,\n        chunk: None,\n        source_url: Some(\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\".to_string()),\n        source_size_bytes: Some(source_size_bytes),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a test reshard backfill job params.\nfn reshard_backfill_params(old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> JobParams {\n    JobParams {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        primary_key: None,\n        shard_count: None,\n        old_shards: Some(old_shards),\n        target_shards: Some(target_shards),\n test_acceptance_1gb_dump_splits_into_4_chunks() {\n    // Acceptance: 1 GB dump: first pod splits into 4× 256 MiB chunks\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-1\");\n\n    // Enqueue a 1GB dump import job\n    let params = dump_import_params(1_073_741_824); // 1 GiB\n    let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n\n    // Claim the job\n    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n    assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n\n    // Split into chunks (4 chunks of ~256 MiB each)\n    let chunk_size = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n    // Ceiling division: (size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size\n    let total_chunks = ((1_073_741_824 + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size) as u32;\n\n    let chunks: Vec<_> = (0..total_chunks)\n        .map(|i| {\n            let i = i as u64;\n            let start = i * chunk_size;\n            let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size, 1_073_741_824u64);\n            crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk {\n                index: i as u32,\n                total: total_chunks,\n                start: start.to_string(),\n                end: end.to_string(),\n                size_bytes: end - start,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let chunk_ids = coord.split_job_into_chunks(&claimed, chunks).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(chunk_ids.len(), 4);\n\n    // Verify chunks are queued\n    let child_jobs = coord.list_chunks(&job_id).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(child_jobs.len(), 4);\n\n    // Verify all chunks are in queued state\n    for child in &child_jobs {\n        assert_eq!(child.state, \"queued\");\n        assert_eq!(child.parent_job_id, Some(job_id.clone()));\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_acceptance_claim_expires_after_30s() {\n    // Acceptance: Kill a claimant mid-chunk: claim expires in 30s;\n    // another pod picks up and resumes at last_cursor\n    let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n\n    let coord1 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-1\", store.clone());\n\n    // Enqueue a job\n    let params = dump_import_params(1_000_000_000);\n    let job_id = coord1.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n\n    // Pod 1 claims the job\n    let claimed = coord1.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n\n    // Update progress to simulate some work done\n    let progress = JobProgress {\n        bytes_processed: 500_000_000,\n        docs_routed: 5000,\n        last_cursor: \"500000000\".to_string(),\n        error: None,\n    };\n    coord1.update_progress(&job_id, &progress, JobState::InProgress).unwrap();\n\n    // Manually set the claim expiration to the past to simulate time passing\n    // In a real scenario, the pod would crash and stop renewing\n    let expired_time = now_ms() - 1000; // 1 second ago\n    coord1.set_claim_expires_at_for_test(&job_id, expired_time).unwrap();\n\n    // Verify the claim is now expired\n    let job = coord1.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n    assert!(job.claim_expires_at.unwrap() < now_ms());\n\n    // Create a second coordinator representing another pod with the SAME store\n    let coord2 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-2\", store);\n\n    // Reclaim expired claims\n    let reclaimed = coord2.reclaim_expired_claims().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(reclaimed, 1); // Should reclaim pod-1's expired claim\n\n    // Verify the job is back in queued state\n    let job = coord2.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(job.state, \"queued\");\n    assert!(job.claimed_by.is_none());\n\n    // Pod 2 can now claim the job\n    let claimed2 = coord2.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should reclaim job\");\n    assert_eq!(claimed2.id, job_id);\n    assert_eq!(claimed2.claimed_by, \"pod-2\");\n\n    // Verify progress was preserved for idempotent resume\n    let reclaimed_progress = claimed2.parse_progress().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(reclaimed_progress.last_cursor, \"500000000\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_acceptance_hpa_queue_depth_metric() {\n    // Acceptance: HPA on miroir_background_queue_depth > 10 triggers scale-up\n    // during the burst; scale-down once empty\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-metrics\");\n\n    // Initially empty\n    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n\n    // Enqueue 15 jobs (above HPA threshold of 10)\n    for i in 0..15 {\n        let params = dump_import_params(1_000_000_000);\n        let job_id = format!(\"job-{}\", i);\n        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Queue depth should be 15\n    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 15);\n\n    // Claim 5 jobs\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim\");\n    }\n\n    // Queue depth should now be 10 (at HPA threshold)\n    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 10);\n\n    // Complete the remaining jobs\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        if let Some(claimed) = coord.claim_job().unwrap() {\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            coord.complete_job(&claimed.id, &progress).unwrap();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Queue depth should be 5\n    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 5);\n\n    // Claim and complete remaining jobs\n    while let Some(claimed) = coord.claim_job().unwrap() {\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        coord.complete_job(&claimed.id, &progress).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Queue should be empty (scale-down condition)\n    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_acceptance_two_concurrent_dumps_interleave() {\n    // Acceptance: Two concurrent dumps: chunks from both interleave test_acceptance_three_pods_claim_chunks_in_parallel() {\n    // Acceptance: 3 pods claim 3 of 4 chunks in parallel; queue drains\n    // Tests that multiple pods can claim chunks from the same parent job\n    let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n\n    // Enqueue a 1GB dump import job\n    let params = dump_import_params(1_073_741_824); // 1 GiB\n    let job_id = {\n        let coord = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-1\", store.clone());\n        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap()\n    };\n\n    // Pod 1 claims the job and splits it into 4 chunks\n    let coord1 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-1\", store.clone());\n    let claimed = coord1.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"pod-1 should claim job\");\n    assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n\n    let chunk_size = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n    let total_chunks = 4;\n\n    let chunks: Vec<_> = (0..total_chunks)\n        .map(|i| {\n            let i = i as u64;\n            let start = i * chunk_size;\n            let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size, 1_073_741_824u64);\n            crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk {\n                index: i as u32,\n                total: total_chunks,\n                start: start.to_string(),\n                end: end.to_string(),\n                size_bytes: end - start,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    coord1.split_job_into_chunks(&claimed, chunks).unwrap();\n\n    // Verify 4 chunks are queued\n    let child_jobs = coord1.list_chunks(&job_id).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(child_jobs.len(), 4);\n    assert_eq!(coord1.queue_depth().unwrap(), 4);\n\n    // Create 3 coordinators representing 3 different pods\n    let coord2 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-2\", store.clone());\n    let coord3 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-3\", store.clone());\n\n    // Pod 1 claims a chunk\n    let claimed1 = coord1.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"pod-1 should claim a chunk\");\n    assert!(claimed1.parent_job_id.is_some()); // It's a chunk job\n    assert_eq!(coord1.queue_depth().unwrap(), 3);\n\n    // Pod 2 claims a chunk\n    let claimed2 = coord2.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"pod-2 should claim a chunk\");\n    assert!(claimed2.parent_job_id.is_some());\n    assert_ne!(claimed1.id, claimed2.id); // Different chunks\n    assert_eq!(coord2.queue_depth().unwrap(), 2);\n\n    // Pod 3 claims a chunk\n    let claimed3 = coord3.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"pod-3 should claim a chunk\");\n    assert!(claimed3.parent_job_id.is_some());\n    assert_ne!(claimed2.id, claimed3.id); // Different chunks\n    assert_ne!(claimed1.id, claimed3.id); // Different chunks\n    assert_eq!(coord3.queue_depth().unwrap(), 1);\n\n    // 1 chunk remains queued\n    assert_eq!(coord1.queue_depth().unwrap(), 1);\n\n    // Pods complete their chunks\n    let progress = JobProgress::default();\n    coord1.complete_job(&claimed1.id, &progress).unwrap();\n    coord2.complete_job(&claimed2.id, &progress).unwrap();\n    coord3.complete_job(&claimed3.id, &progress).unwrap();\n\n    // Queue depth should be 1 (remaining chunk)\n    assert_eq!(coord1.queue_depth().unwrap(), 1);\n\n    // Pod 1 claims and completes the final chunk\n    let claimed4 = coord1.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"pod-1 should claim final chunk\");\n    coord1.complete_job(&claimed4.id, &progress).unwrap();\n\n    // Queue is now drained\n    assert_eq!(coord1.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_acceptance_reshard_backfill_chunking() {\n    // Acceptance: Reshard backfill with 64 old shards splits into chunks\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-reshard\");\n\n    // Enqueue a reshard job: 64 -> 128 shards\n    let params = reshard_backfill_params(64, 128);\n    let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::ReshardBackfill, params).unwrap();\n\n    // Claim the job\n    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n\n    // Split into chunks by shard-id range\n    use crate::reshard_chunking;\n\n    let specs = reshard_chunking::split_reshard_into_chunks(64, 128, 16);\n    let chunks = reshard_chunking::reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs);\n\n    assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 4); // 64 shards / 16 per chunk = 4 chunks\n\n    coord.split_job_into_chunks(&claimed, chunks).unwrap();\n\n    // Verify chunks\n    let child_jobs = coord.list_chunks(&job_id).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(child_jobs.len(), 4);\n\n    // Verify each chunk has the correct shard range\n    assert_eq!(child_jobs[0].chunk_index, Some(0));\n test_acceptance_claim_heartbeat_renewal() {\n    // Test that claim heartbeat extends the expiration\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-heartbeat\");\n\n    // Enqueue and claim a job\n    let params = dump_import_params(1_000_000_000);\n    let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n\n tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10)).await;\n\n    // Renew the claim\n    let renewed = coord.renew_claim(&job_id).unwrap();\n    assert!(renewed);\n\n    let job = coord.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n    let new_expires_at = job.claim_expires_at.unwrap();\n\n    // Expiration should be extended (at least 10ms later due to our sleep)\n    assert!(new_expires_at > original_expires_at);\n\n    // Should be approximately 30 seconds from now\n    let now = now_ms();\n    assert!(new_expires_at > now);\n    assert!(new_expires_at <= now + 30_000);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_acceptance_chunk_job_progress_tracking() {\n    // Test that chunk jobs can track progress independently\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-progress\");\n\n    // and per-node shard distribution\n810\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n811\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) n.is_healthy())\n830\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n831\t                if healthy {\n832\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n833\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n834\t                }\n835\t            }\n836\t        }\n837\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n838\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n839\t        } else {\n840\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n841\t        }\n842\t    }\n843\t\n844\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n845\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n846\t    } else {\n847\t        1.0\n848\t    };\n849\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n850\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n851\t\n852\t    for (node_id, resource-pressure gauges.\n858\t///\n859\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n860\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n861\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n862\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n863\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n864\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n865\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n866\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n867\t\n868\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n869\t        if max > 0 {\n870\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n871\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n872\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n873\t        }\n874\t    }\n875\t\n876\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n877\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n878\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n879\t     // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n883\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n884\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n885\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n886\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n887\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n888\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n889\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n890\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n891\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n892\t                }\n893\t            }\n894\t        }\n895\t    }\n896\t\n897\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n898\t    // Peer pod count is now set by peer discovery refresh loop (plan §14.5).\n899\t    // Leader election is not yet implemented (plan §14.5 Mode B).\n900\t    // Owned shards count will be set by Mode A rendezvous (plan §14.5).\n901\t    metrics.set_leader(\"global\", true);\n902\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n903\t}\n904\t\n905\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n906\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n907\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n908\t        .ok()\n909\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n910\t}\n911\t\n912\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n913\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n914\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n915\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n916\t            .await\n917\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C tokio::select! {\n932\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n933\t        _ = terminate => {},\n934\t    }\n935\t\n936\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n937\t\n938\t
